questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Sections' feature in MS PowerPoint when managing a large presentation?
questions.option_1: To create hyperlinks between different parts of the presentation
questions.option_2: To divide the presentation into logical groupings for better organization and navigation during editing and presenting
questions.option_3: To apply different themes to specific groups of slides
questions.option_4: To enable collaborative editing of specific parts of the presentation by different users

questions.question: One important outcome of British social reforms was ______.
questions.option_2: emergence of women in public life

questions.question: How much international bandwidth does Airtel's 2Africa Pearls 2025 undersea cable landing system add?
What type of engine is used in the Hansa NG trainer aircraft launched in April 2025?
In most Unix-based operating systems, which command is used to create an empty file if the file does not already exist?

Consider a scenario where a user is interacting with a virtual reality (VR) environment. Which of the following input devices is least likely to be the primary means of navigation and interaction within this immersive environment?
questions.option_2: Optical mouse connected to the computer
questions.option_4: Head-mounted display with integrated motion tracking

questions.question: Where do deleted files go by default in Windows OS?
Which element on the Windows 11 desktop allows users to quickly access multiple open windows and virtual desktops?
Which of the following methods is used to create a new folder in Windows 11 OS from Desktop?
Which of the following methods is the simplest way to rename a file or folder in Windows?
questions.option_1: Double-click the file to open it
questions.option_2: Drag the file to the Recycle Bin

questions.question: In many Windows applications, pressing Alt + Spacebar typically invokes which of the following?
questions.option_3: The system menu for the current window (containing options like Move, Size, Minimize, Maximize, Close)

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut permanently deletes a file without sending it to the Recycle Bin in Windows OS?
Which function key can be used with Alt to close the current application in Windows applications?
Which keyboard shortcut opens File Explorer in Windows 11 OS to navigate and create files/folders?
Which of the following statements about primary and secondary memory is correct in a computer system?
questions.option_1: Primary memory is used for long-term storage, while secondary memory is used for temporary storage of data.
questions.option_2: Both primary and secondary memory are directly accessible by the CPU for processing instructions.
questions.option_3: Primary memory is faster but has lower storage capacity, while secondary memory is slower but provides large storage capacity.
questions.option_4: Secondary memory is volatile, meaning data is lost when power is turned off, while primary memory is non-volatile and retains data.

questions.question: Which of the following technologies was used to fabricate the Vikram 3201 microprocessor launched in 2025?
In the context of managing multiple open applications within a graphical operating system, what is the typical behaviour initiated by pressing Ctrl + Tab?
questions.option_1: It cycles through the open windows or tabs of the currently active application.
questions.option_2: It opens a new tab or window within the currently active application.
questions.option_3: It closes the currently active application window.
questions.option_4: It minimises the currently active application window to the taskbar or dock.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an input device that allows users to interact with a computer by typing text?
In the context of computer memory hierarchy, which of the following statements regarding access speed and cost per bit is generally true as you move from registers towards secondary storage?
questions.option_1: Access speed decreases and cost per bit decreases
questions.option_2: Access speed decreases and cost per bit increases.
questions.option_3: Access speed increases and cost per bit increases
questions.option_4: Access speed increases and cost per bit decreases

questions.question: Which of the following keyboard shortcuts is used to insert a new slide in PowerPoint 2019?
Which keyboard shortcut opens the Windows 11 OS Settings menu to change system settings?
Which of the following is the keyboard shortcut to save an MS Excel workbook?
Which of the following actions is performed when the Ctrl + V shortcut is used in most computer applications?
questions.option_1: Opens the new file creation dialog box
questions.option_2: Paste the copied or cut content

questions.question: Which of the following is an incorrect statement about Cache Memory?
questions.option_1: Cache memory is a form of secondary storage.
questions.option_2: Cache memory is located close to or inside the CPU.
questions.option_3: Cache memory is faster than main memory.
questions.option_4: Cache memory stores frequently accessed data for quick retrieval.

questions.question: Which of the following abbreviations represents a volatile memory technology commonly used for the primary working memory in personal computers?
